definition: Weird and sinister or ghostly; unnervingly strange.
examples: An eldritch screech echoed from the depths of the ancient well.
The eldritch glow of the fungi illuminated the cavern walls.

definition: Too great or extreme to be expressed or described in words.
examples: She felt an ineffable joy upon seeing the northern lights for the first time.
The ineffable complexity of the cosmos humbled even the greatest minds.

definition: Optimistic or positive, especially in a difficult situation; blood-red.
examples: He remained sanguine despite the cascade of setbacks.
The sanguine hue of the desert sunset stained the horizon.

definition: Dark; shadowy or obscure.
examples: They descended into the tenebrous passages beneath the old cathedral.
His tenebrous past was a subject no one dared broach.

definition: Forming a very small remnant of something that was once much larger or more noticeable.
examples: The vestigial remains of the fortress crumbled against the cliffside.
A vestigial sense of duty compelled him to return.

definition: A whispering or rustling sound.
examples: The susurrus of the wind through the pines was the only sound for miles.
A susurrus of hushed voices filled the dim hall.

definition: Softly bright or radiant; running lightly over a surface.
examples: The lambent flames of the hearth cast dancing shadows on the stone walls.
Her lambent wit charmed even the most taciturn of guests.

definition: Associated with death; gruesomely reminiscent of a place where corpses are kept.
examples: The charnel stench rising from the bog was unbearable.
They stumbled upon a charnel pit hidden beneath the ruined chapel.

definition: Translucently clear; easily understood.
examples: The pellucid waters of the mountain lake revealed every stone on the bottom.
His pellucid prose made even the most complex ideas accessible.

definition: Relating to or characterized by reversion to something ancient or ancestral.
examples: An atavistic fear gripped him as the forest closed in around the path.
The ritual had an atavistic quality, echoing rites older than memory.

definition: To flash or sparkle; to give off brilliant flashes of light.
examples: The gemstones coruscated under the lamplight, scattering prismatic shards across the ceiling.
Lightning coruscated along the ridgeline, illuminating the barren plain.

definition: Of, resembling, or relating to twilight.
examples: Crepuscular light filtered through the canopy, painting the trail in shades of amber.
The crepuscular hours were his favourite time to walk the moors.

definition: Unable to be placated; relentless and unstoppable.
examples: The implacable advance of the desert swallowed village after village.
She was implacable in her pursuit of the truth, regardless of the cost.

definition: Having a strong religious or spiritual quality; indicating the presence of a divinity.
examples: The ancient grove had a numinous quality that silenced even the most sceptical visitors.
A numinous hush fell over the congregation as the chant began.

definition: Something reused or altered but still bearing visible traces of its earlier form.
examples: The city was a palimpsest of civilisations, each layer visible beneath the next.
Her face was a palimpsest of emotions—grief written over joy, exhaustion over resolve.

definition: In a state or period of inactivity or dormancy.
examples: The volcano had been quiescent for decades, but tremors now hinted at its awakening.
He sat quiescent by the fire, his thoughts turning inward.

definition: Flashing like lightning; dazzlingly bright.
examples: A fulgurant bolt split the sky and struck the ancient oak.
His fulgurant insight cut through the confusion in the room.

definition: Not likely to provoke dissent or offence; serving to alleviate pain.
examples: The politician gave an anodyne speech that carefully avoided any controversy.
Music was his anodyne against the grinding stress of daily life.

definition: Occupying a position at, or on both sides of, a boundary or threshold.
examples: The hallway had a liminal quality, belonging neither to the house nor the garden.
He existed in a liminal state between sleep and waking.

definition: A tiny trace or spark of a specified quality or feeling.
examples: There was not a scintilla of evidence to support the accusation.
A scintilla of hope flickered in the darkness of her despair.

definition: To be harmed or destroyed by one's own scheme or device intended for others.
examples: The lobbyist was hoist with his own petard when the leaked emails exposed his own corruption.
She tried to undermine her rival but was hoist with her own petard when the plan backfired.

definition: The most perfect or typical example of a quality or class; the purest essence.
examples: He was the quintessence of elegance, effortless in every gesture.
The dish was the quintessence of simplicity—three ingredients, perfectly balanced.

definition: Of the same blood or origin; related by birth.
examples: The consanguineous ties between the two houses made the feud all the more tragic.
Their consanguineous bond was undeniable, visible in every shared mannerism.

definition: Opposing; antagonistic; combative.
examples: The two factions remained oppugnant, each refusing to yield an inch.
His oppugnant nature made diplomacy an exercise in futility.

definition: Conducive to success; favourable. Giving or being a sign of future success.
examples: The clear skies on launch day seemed auspicious for the mission.
It was not the most auspicious beginning—the keynote speaker tripped on stage.

word: Brevity is the soul of wit
definition: Intelligent and effective communication is best achieved through conciseness.
examples: His code reviews were legendary—brevity is the soul of wit, and his comments never exceeded a sentence.
She trimmed the report from forty pages to ten, proving that brevity is the soul of wit.

definition: A period of youth, inexperience, and naïve idealism.
examples: In his salad days he believed every startup pitch he heard.
Those were her salad days—reckless, hopeful, and blissfully unaware of consequence.

definition: A crimson or pinkish-red colour; to dye or stain red.
examples: The setting sun incarnadined the snow-capped peaks.
A single drop of ink fell and incarnadined the entire basin of water.

definition: Reduced to a state of poverty; exhausted the resources or ability of.
examples: The spectacle beggared description—no photograph could do it justice.
Years of mismanagement had beggared the once-prosperous institution.

definition: Given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behaviour.
examples: The capricious weather turned a sunny hike into a drenched retreat.
Investors fled from the capricious policy shifts of the new administration.

definition: Wild and noisy disorder or confusion; the capital of Hell in Milton's Paradise Lost.
examples: Pandemonium erupted in the stadium when the underdog scored in stoppage time.
The open-plan office descended into pandemonium the moment the servers went down.

definition: Extremely dark, gloomy, or forbidding; of or relating to the river Styx.
examples: The stygian depths of the ocean trench admitted no light whatsoever.
A stygian silence fell over the battlefield after the last shot.

definition: Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or course of action; hardened against feeling.
examples: The obdurate negotiator refused every compromise put before him.
Despite the mounting evidence, she remained obdurate in her original assessment.

definition: The highest part of heaven, regarded as a realm of pure fire or light; celestial.
examples: The composer's final symphony seemed to reach into the empyrean itself.
From the summit, the view was empyrean—boundless sky in every direction.

definition: A person who renounces a religious or political belief or principle.
examples: The apostate was shunned by the community he had once led.
She was branded an apostate for questioning the founding tenets of the organisation.

definition: Extremely delicate and light in a way that seems not of this world.
examples: The ethereal mist hanging over the lake gave it a dreamlike quality.
Her voice had an ethereal quality that silenced the room.

definition: A state of eternal punishment and damnation; complete ruin.
examples: The gambler's path led inexorably to financial perdition.
He warned them that pride would be their perdition.

definition: Shining brightly; radiant; emanating joy or goodness.
examples: The effulgent dawn broke through weeks of overcast gloom.
Her effulgent smile was enough to disarm even the sternest critic.

definition: Unbreakable; utterly unyielding; having the hardness of diamond.
examples: Her adamantine resolve carried the project through three rounds of rejection.
The adamantine chains of habit are too light to be felt until they are too heavy to be broken.

definition: Characteristic of or resembling a seraph; angelically pure, sublime, or beautiful.
examples: The child gazed up with a seraphic innocence that disarmed every adult in the room.
A seraphic chorus rose from the cathedral, filling the nave with otherworldly sound.
